name: node-browserslist
title: node-browserslist
summary: Share browsers list between different front-end tools
description:
Share browsers list between different front-end tools
This module can be used to share browsers list between different front-end
tools, like Autoprefixer, Stylelint and babel-env-preset.
.
Developers set browsers list in queries like 'last 2 version' to be free from
updating browser versions manually. Browserslist will use 'Can I Use' data for
this queries.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.

commands.name: browserslist
commands.description: The config to share target browsers and Node.js versions

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use browserslist because Share browsers list between different front-end tools.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use browserslist as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `browserslist  --config="path/to/browserlist/file" npx browserslist --coverage "QUERIES"` as documented upstream for browserslist.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `browserslist --ignore-unknown-versions` as documented upstream for browserslist.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair browserslist with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `browserslist --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: browserslist - The config to share target browsers and Node.js versions
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install node-browserslist. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `browserslist  --config="path/to/browserlist/file" npx browserslist --coverage "QUERIES"`. Further invocations from upstream: `browserslist --ignore-unknown-versions`. Share browsers list between different front-end tools.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Share browsers list between different front-end tools.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: browserslist - The config to share target browsers and Node.js versions.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install node-browserslist
Install with: sudo apt install node-browserslist
